results.name: orchestrator
results.description:
End-to-end ResCon outbound pipeline intended to run on a Temporal Schedule
(see ``apps/service/scripts/apply_schedules.py``). No manual trigger_input
beyond ``phone_number_id`` + ``zen_workflow_id`` — eligible contacts come
from Salesforce, so rows are NOT user-supplied.

Pipeline:
  1. subflow    → pre-call-pipeline (Salesforce eligibility pipeline) → rows
  2. transform  → shape rows into ``{contacts}`` envelope for batch_call
  3. batch_call → fan-out dials (empty-contacts guard is handled inside
                  BatchCallWorkflow — it short-circuits to a zero-result
                  envelope, so no explicit guard node is needed here)
  4. subflow    → post-call-pipeline (SF VoiceCall + Case close per row,
                  branching on call outcome: confirmed vs no-contact)
  5. response   → summary counts surfaced to the run detail page

Co-located with pre-call-pipeline so subflow resolution stays sibling-by-name
(the engine's subflow handler rejects cross-suite filesystem refs).
results.definition_yaml:
name: rescon_outbound_scheduled
description: |
  End-to-end ResCon outbound pipeline intended to run on a Temporal Schedule
  (see ``apps/service/scripts/apply_schedules.py``). No manual trigger_input
  beyond ``phone_number_id`` + ``zen_workflow_id`` — eligible contacts come
  from Salesforce, so rows are NOT user-supplied.

  Pipeline:
    1. subflow    → pre-call-pipeline (Salesforce eligibility pipeline) → rows
    2. transform  → shape rows into ``{contacts}`` envelope for batch_call
    3. batch_call → fan-out dials (empty-contacts guard is handled inside
                    BatchCallWorkflow — it short-circuits to a zero-result
                    envelope, so no explicit guard node is needed here)
    4. subflow    → post-call-pipeline (SF VoiceCall + Case close per row,
                    branching on call outcome: confirmed vs no-contact)
    5. response   → summary counts surfaced to the run detail page

  Co-located with pre-call-pipeline so subflow resolution stays sibling-by-name
  (the engine's subflow handler rejects cross-suite filesystem refs).

trigger:
  type: manual
  input_schema:
    phone_number_id:
      type: integer
      required: true
    zen_workflow_id:
      type: integer
      required: true
    only_phone:
      # Optional single-contact filter for manual testing. When set, node 2
      # filters the pre-call-pipeline output down to the one row whose ``phone``
      # matches (E.164, exact string compare). The scheduler always omits this
      # so production runs dial every eligible contact.
      type: string
      required: false
    dry_run:
      type: boolean
      required: false

    # Optional check-in date window — forwarded down through pre-call-pipeline
    # to soql-open-cases's SOQL WHERE clause. Accepts SOQL date literals
    # (``TODAY``, ``NEXT_N_DAYS:30``) or ISO date strings (``2026-06-01``).
    # Defaults live in soql-open-cases's normalizer node (``TODAY`` /
    # ``2099-12-31``), which reproduce the pre-parameterization behavior when
    # either key is omitted.
    check_in_start:
      type: string
      required: false
    check_in_end:
      type: string
      required: false

    # Post-call wrap-up metadata — supplied per tenant at schedule-apply
    # time. Forwarded to node 99 (post-call-pipeline composite) which passes
    # them down to the confirmed/no-contact close atoms. All optional;
    # missing values simply produce a thinner SF VoiceCall record.
    from_phone:
      # E.164 Twilio number the dial originates from. Now auto-derived
      # from ``phone_number_id`` via voice-py's enrichment signal
      # (collected_variables.from_phone). Only needed as an override
      # if the Twilio number differs from the one voice-py resolved.
      type: string
      required: false
    call_center_id:
      # SF CallCenter.Id. GPR prod uses "04vSu00000JGXgDIAX".
      type: string
      required: false
    call_source:
      type: string
      default: "OS"
      required: false
    call_disposition:
      type: string
      default: "OS: ResCon"
      required: false
    # call_detail_1 removed — now outcome-dependent, computed inside
    # post-call-pipeline's node 1 transform (confirmed → "OS: ResCon Success",
    # no_contact → "OS: ResCon No Contact").

nodes:
  - id: 1
    type: subflow
    title: Fetch eligible callables from Salesforce (pre-call-pipeline)
    workflow_file: ../pre-call-pipeline/workflow.yaml
    # Forward the optional date window down to pre-call-pipeline →
    # soql-open-cases's SOQL. The normalizer handles the
    # undefined-or-empty fallback, so passing {{ input.* }} verbatim
    # here is safe even when the scheduler (or Run dialog) omits both keys.
    input:
      check_in_start: "{{ input.check_in_start }}"
      check_in_end:   "{{ input.check_in_end }}"

  - id: 2
    type: transform
    title: Shape rows into {contacts} envelope for batch_call
    description: |
      Maps pre-call-pipeline's flat row shape into batch_call's expected
      ``{contacts: [{phone, variables}]}`` envelope. Every Salesforce key
      the agent script or the post-call subflow (post-call-pipeline) needs
      rides inside ``variables`` so it reaches voice-py verbatim.
    input:
      # pre-call-pipeline's response body is now a dict:
      #   { callables: [...rows...], counts: {...}, deferred_multi_date: [...] }
      # ``subflow_handler`` unwraps the response body into ``nodes.1.output``
      # (not ``nodes.1.output.body`` — that pattern is only for ``nango_proxy``
      # nodes, where the HTTP response body lives alongside status/headers).
      rows: "{{ nodes.1.output.callables }}"
      only_phone: "{{ input.only_phone }}"
    # Outer [] forces array context — $map over an empty list returns
    # undefined (→ None) otherwise, which would poison batch_call's
    # iterate_over template with a non-iterable. Keep as list, always.
    #
    # ``only_phone`` is a manual-test knob. When the trigger_input carries
    # a non-empty string, we slice ``rows`` down to the single matching
    # contact before mapping; the scheduler omits it so production runs
    # dial everyone.
    expression: |
      (
        $filtered := only_phone and only_phone != "" ? $filter(rows, function($r) { $r.phone = only_phone }) : rows;
        {
          "contacts": [$map($filtered, function($r) {
            {
              "phone": $r.phone,
              "variables": {
                "contact_name":    $r.first_name & " " & $r.last_name,
                "first_name":      $r.first_name,
                "last_name":       $r.last_name,
                "account_id":      $r.account_id,
                "account_name":    $r.account_name,
                "contact_id":      $r.contact_id,
                "email":           $r.email,
                "case_id":         $r.case_id,
                "case_number":     $r.case_number,
                "subject":         $r.subject,
                "city":            $r.city,
                "check_in_date":   $r.check_in_date,
                "check_out_date":  $r.check_out_date,
                "resort":          $r.resort,
                "contract_id":     $r.contract_id,
                "sum_past_due":    $r.sum_past_due,
                "customer_type":                  $r.customer_type,
                "account_owner_type":             $r.account_owner_type,
                "resort_name_api":                $r.resort,
                "customer_first_name_api":        $r.first_name,
                "customer_reservation_dates_api": $fromMillis($toMillis($r.check_in_date, "[Y]-[M01]-[D01]"), "[MNn] [D1o]") & " to " & $fromMillis($toMillis($r.check_out_date, "[Y]-[M01]-[D01]"), "[MNn] [D1o]"),
                "customer_type_api":              $r.customer_type ? $r.customer_type : "owner"
              }
            }
          })],
          "total": $count($filtered)
        }
      )

  - id: 3
    type: batch_call
    title: Fan out per-contact dials
    iterate_over: "{{ nodes.2.output.contacts }}"
    item_alias: contact
    concurrency: 5
    child_workflow: outbound_call
    phone_number_id: "{{ input.phone_number_id }}"
    zen_workflow_id: "{{ input.zen_workflow_id }}"
    child_input:
      phone: "{{ contact.phone }}"
      variables: "{{ contact.variables }}"
    child_execution_timeout_seconds: 900
    child_run_timeout_seconds: 600

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Node 99 — Post-call Salesforce writeback (composite subflow).
  #
  # Dispatches to the confirmed / no-contact close atoms per row based on
  # ``result.success``. See ../post-call-pipeline/workflow.yaml for the
  # branching logic; _atoms/close-case-{confirmed,no-contact} for the
  # individual VoiceCall + Case PATCH payloads.
  #
  # Wrap-up metadata (from_phone, call_center_id, call_source/disposition/
  # detail_1) is identical across all rows in one campaign run, so pass it
  # once at the composite input boundary rather than per-row in variables.
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  - id: 99
    type: subflow
    title: Post-call Salesforce writeback
    workflow_file: ../post-call-pipeline/workflow.yaml
    input:
      results:          "{{ nodes.3.output.results }}"
      # Campaign-scoped metadata. ``from_phone`` is auto-derived from
      # voice-py's enrichment signal; call_center_id is per-tenant.
      # call_source / call_disposition have safe defaults in post-call-pipeline's transform.
      # call_detail_1 is outcome-dependent (computed inside post-call-pipeline, not passed).
      from_phone:       "{{ input.from_phone }}"
      call_center_id:   "{{ input.call_center_id }}"
      call_source:      "{{ input.call_source }}"
      call_disposition: "{{ input.call_disposition }}"

  - id: 4
    type: response
    title: Summary counts (pre-call → dial → post-call)
    body:
      # Pre-call layer (pre-call-pipeline Phase 1 gate)
      soql_matches:         "{{ nodes.1.output.counts.total_soql_matches }}"
      distinct_accounts:    "{{ nodes.1.output.counts.distinct_accounts }}"
      phase1_eligible:      "{{ nodes.1.output.counts.phase1_eligible }}"
      deferred_multi_date:  "{{ nodes.1.output.counts.deferred_multi_date }}"
      # Dial layer (batch_call)
      total:                "{{ nodes.3.output.total }}"
      completed:            "{{ nodes.3.output.completed }}"
      failed:               "{{ nodes.3.output.failed }}"
      total_call_seconds:   "{{ $sum(nodes.3.output.results.duration_seconds) }}"
      # Post-call writeback layer (post-call-pipeline composite)
      confirmed_count:      "{{ nodes.99.output.confirmed_count }}"
      no_contact_count:     "{{ nodes.99.output.no_contact_count }}"
      skipped_count:        "{{ nodes.99.output.skipped_count }}"
      voice_calls_created:  "{{ nodes.99.output.voice_calls_created }}"
      post_call_rows:       "{{ nodes.99.output.rows }}"
      # Per-call collected variables from the enrichment signal — surfaced
      # here so the run detail page shows what the bot collected per contact.
      call_results:         "{{ nodes.3.output.results }}"

edges:
  - from: 1
    to: 2
  - from: 2
    to: 3
  - from: 3
    to: 99
  - from: 99
    to: 4

results.name: post-call-pipeline
results.description:
End-to-end post-call writeback composite. Consumes batch_call's per-row
result envelope from the orchestrator, branches per row on call outcome,
and dispatches to the correct atom:

  reservation_confirmed=true           -> _atoms/close-case-confirmed
  not answered (no_answer/busy/failed) -> _atoms/close-case-no-contact
  answered but not confirmed           -> skipped (no case closure)
    (callback_requested, declined + transferred, wrong number)

Branching uses collected_variables from the enrichment signal, not just
the binary success flag. An answered call where the owner declined or
requested a callback should NOT be closed as "RC: Confirmed".

Pipeline:
  1. transform -> normalize batch_call results: compute outcome using
                 collected_variables, split into closeable + skipped
  2. loop      -> for each closeable result, dispatch to the correct atom
  3. transform -> roll up per-row outcomes into a single summary
  4. response  -> return aggregate counts + per-row branch decisions
results.definition_yaml:
name: post_call_pipeline
description: |
  End-to-end post-call writeback composite. Consumes batch_call's per-row
  result envelope from the orchestrator, branches per row on call outcome,
  and dispatches to the correct atom:

    reservation_confirmed=true           -> _atoms/close-case-confirmed
    not answered (no_answer/busy/failed) -> _atoms/close-case-no-contact
    answered but not confirmed           -> skipped (no case closure)
      (callback_requested, declined + transferred, wrong number)

  Branching uses collected_variables from the enrichment signal, not just
  the binary success flag. An answered call where the owner declined or
  requested a callback should NOT be closed as "RC: Confirmed".

  Pipeline:
    1. transform -> normalize batch_call results: compute outcome using
                   collected_variables, split into closeable + skipped
    2. loop      -> for each closeable result, dispatch to the correct atom
    3. transform -> roll up per-row outcomes into a single summary
    4. response  -> return aggregate counts + per-row branch decisions

trigger:
  type: manual
  input_schema:
    # batch_call result envelope, passed in by the orchestrator.
    # Shape (per-row inside `results`):
    #   { phone, success: bool, duration_seconds, status, error,
    #     variables: { case_id, account_id, ... }, call_sid, ... }
    results:
      type: array
      required: true

    # Per-call metadata that's identical across all rows in one campaign run
    # (resort + agent identity supplied by voice-py / the running instance).
    # These can be left null and the atoms will pass through; the post-call
    # SF write will still succeed but the SF wrap-up record will lack them.
    from_phone:
      type: string
      required: false
    call_center_id:
      type: string
      required: false
    call_source:
      # Campaign-level label. Fallback "OS" applied in node 1 transform
      # if omitted or empty (engine does not resolve input_schema defaults).
      type: string
      required: false
    call_disposition:
      # Campaign-level label. Fallback "OS: ResCon" applied in node 1.
      type: string
      required: false
    # call_detail_1 is NOT an input — it's outcome-dependent, computed
    # per-row in node 1's transform:
    #   confirmed  → "OS: ResCon Success"
    #   no_contact → "OS: ResCon No Contact"
    #   skipped    → "OS: ResCon" (generic, no atom dispatched)

connections:
  salesforce:
    provider_config_key: salesforce
    connection_id: 70959a87-160b-439b-967f-aa0146c4b112

nodes:
  - id: 1
    type: transform
    title: Normalize batch_call results — outcome-based branching
    description: |
      Computes per-row outcome using collected_variables from the
      enrichment signal, NOT just the binary ``success`` flag:

        reservation_confirmed=true  -> "confirmed"  (close-case-confirmed)
        success=false               -> "no_contact" (close-case-no-contact)
        answered but not confirmed  -> "skipped"    (no case closure)

      An answered call where the owner declined, requested a callback,
      was a wrong number, or was transferred to an agent should NOT be
      closed as "RC: Confirmed" — those paths are handled by the live
      agent or will be re-dialed.

      Output is an object with two arrays:
        closeable — rows that need an atom dispatch (confirmed + no_contact)
        skipped   — answered calls that don't get automated case closure

      The loop (node 2) iterates only over ``closeable``. The summary
      (node 3) counts both for the full picture.
    input:
      results:          "{{ input.results }}"
      # Campaign-scoped metadata — same for every row. Must be stamped onto
      # each row here because the loop handler passes trigger_input={} to
      # child nodes, so ``{{ input.X }}`` is null inside the loop body.
      from_phone:       "{{ input.from_phone }}"
      call_center_id:   "{{ input.call_center_id }}"
      call_source:      "{{ input.call_source }}"
      call_disposition: "{{ input.call_disposition }}"
    expression: |
      (
        $enriched := [$map(results, function($r) {
          (
            $confirmed := $r.collected_variables.reservation_confirmed = true;
            $answered  := $r.success = true;
            $callback  := $r.collected_variables.callback_requested = true;
            $outcome   := $confirmed          ? "confirmed"
                        : $answered = false   ? "no_contact"
                        :                       "skipped";
            $atom      := $outcome = "confirmed"  ? "close-case-confirmed"
                        : $outcome = "no_contact" ? "close-case-no-contact"
                        :                           null;
            {
              "outcome":         $outcome,
              "atom_name":       $atom,
              "case_id":         $r.variables.case_id,
              "account_id":      $r.variables.account_id,
              "to_phone":        $r.phone,
              "from_phone":      $r.collected_variables.from_phone ? $r.collected_variables.from_phone : from_phone,
              "call_sid":        $r.call_sid,
              "call_timestamp":  $r.completed_at ? $r.completed_at : $now(),
              "resort":          $r.variables.resort,
              "summary":         $r.collected_variables.summary ? $r.collected_variables.summary : ($r.collected_variables.transcript ? $r.collected_variables.transcript : ""),
              "transcript":      $r.collected_variables.transcript ? $r.collected_variables.transcript : "",
              "description":     $r.collected_variables.description ? $r.collected_variables.description : "",
              "callback_requested":    $callback,
              "reservation_confirmed": $confirmed,
              "call_center_id":  call_center_id,
              "call_source":     call_source and call_source != "" ? call_source : "OS",
              "call_disposition": call_disposition and call_disposition != "" ? call_disposition : "OS: ResCon",
              "call_detail_1":   $outcome = "confirmed"  ? "OS: ResCon Success"
                               : $outcome = "no_contact" ? "OS: ResCon No Contact"
                               :                           "OS: ResCon"
            }
          )
        })];
        {
          "closeable":     [$filter($enriched, function($row) { $row.atom_name != null })],
          "skipped":       [$filter($enriched, function($row) { $row.atom_name = null })],
          "skipped_count": $count($filter($enriched, function($row) { $row.atom_name = null })),
          "total":         $count($enriched)
        }
      )

  - id: 2
    type: loop
    title: For each result, branch to the right close atom
    iterate_over: "{{ nodes.1.output.closeable }}"
    item_alias: row
    concurrency: 5
    body:
      # Branch lives in the body via two subflow refs guarded by the
      # ``when`` clause (engine convention — only one fires per iteration).
      # If the engine doesn't support ``when`` on subflows, this collapses
      # to a single transform-then-subflow pattern.
      type: subflow
      # The atom name is pre-computed in node 1's JSONata transform as
      # ``atom_name`` (ternary on success). The template resolver only
      # supports dotted-path lookups — NOT ternary expressions — so we
      # reference the pre-computed field with a simple ``{{ row.atom_name }}``.
      workflow_file: "../_atoms/{{ row.atom_name }}/workflow.yaml"
      input:
        # Confirmed atom needs all 11 fields; no_contact only needs case_id
        # (+ optional notes/owner). Both atoms ignore unknown inputs (the
        # ``input_schema`` only declares what they consume), so passing
        # the union here is safe and keeps the dispatch single-shaped.
        case_id:          "{{ row.case_id }}"
        account_id:       "{{ row.account_id }}"
        from_phone:       "{{ row.from_phone }}"
        to_phone:         "{{ row.to_phone }}"
        call_timestamp:   "{{ row.call_timestamp }}"
        call_sid:         "{{ row.call_sid }}"
        call_center_id:   "{{ row.call_center_id }}"
        resort:           "{{ row.resort }}"
        summary:          "{{ row.summary }}"
        transcript:       "{{ row.transcript }}"
        description:      "{{ row.description }}"
        call_source:      "{{ row.call_source }}"
        call_disposition: "{{ row.call_disposition }}"
        call_detail_1:    "{{ row.call_detail_1 }}"
    collect:
      case_id:           "{{ subflow.output.case_id }}"
      branch:            "{{ subflow.output.branch }}"
      voice_call_id:     "{{ subflow.output.voice_call_id }}"
      voice_call_ok:     "{{ subflow.output.voice_call_ok }}"
      case_close_status: "{{ subflow.output.case_close_status }}"

  - id: 3
    type: transform
    title: Aggregate per-row outcomes into summary counts
    description: |
      Counts confirmed vs no-contact vs skipped closures + VoiceCall
      successes. ``closed_rows`` comes from the loop (node 2) which only
      ran on closeable rows. ``skipped`` comes from node 1's pre-filter.
      ``total`` counts ALL rows so the run detail page tallies correctly.
    input:
      closed_rows:   "{{ nodes.2.output }}"
      skipped:       "{{ nodes.1.output.skipped }}"
      skipped_count: "{{ nodes.1.output.skipped_count }}"
      total:         "{{ nodes.1.output.total }}"
    expression: |
      {
        "rows":                $append(closed_rows, skipped),
        "total":               total,
        "confirmed_count":     $count(closed_rows[branch = "confirmed"]),
        "no_contact_count":    $count(closed_rows[branch = "no_contact"]),
        "skipped_count":       skipped_count,
        "voice_calls_created": $count(closed_rows[voice_call_ok = true])
      }

  - id: 4
    type: response
    title: Return post-call summary
    body: "{{ nodes.3.output }}"

edges:
  - from: 1
    to: 2
  - from: 2
    to: 3
  - from: 3
    to: 4

successes. ``closed_rows`` comes from the loop (node 2) which only
ran on closeable rows. ``skipped`` comes from node 1's pre-filter.
``total`` counts ALL rows so the run detail page tallies correctly.

definition_parsed.description:
End-to-end post-call writeback composite. Consumes batch_call's per-row
result envelope from the orchestrator, branches per row on call outcome,
and dispatches to the correct atom:

  reservation_confirmed=true           -> _atoms/close-case-confirmed
  not answered (no_answer/busy/failed) -> _atoms/close-case-no-contact
  answered but not confirmed           -> skipped (no case closure)
    (callback_requested, declined + transferred, wrong number)

Branching uses collected_variables from the enrichment signal, not just
the binary success flag. An answered call where the owner declined or
requested a callback should NOT be closed as "RC: Confirmed".

Pipeline:
  1. transform -> normalize batch_call results: compute outcome using
                 collected_variables, split into closeable + skipped
  2. loop      -> for each closeable result, dispatch to the correct atom
  3. transform -> roll up per-row outcomes into a single summary
  4. response  -> return aggregate counts + per-row branch decisions

results.name: pre-call-pipeline
results.description:
End-to-end pre-call pipeline. Inputs nothing.

Pipeline:
  01 SOQL query (filters 1..5 enforced at SF)
    -> dedupe by AccountId (group each owner's cases; preserve earliest
      future check-in as primary)
    -> Phase 1 filter: keep only accounts with count(cases) == 1
    -> loop over single-date accounts
        |-- fetch contact for account
        +-- shape eligible-callable row
    -> join with primary case context
    -> response

Phase 1 single-date filter keeps only accounts that have a single open
date in the future so that we don't have to handle multiple dates.
Phase 2 will replace this with a chronological loop across an owner's
future dates.
results.definition_yaml:
name: pre_call_pipeline
description: |
  End-to-end pre-call pipeline. Inputs nothing.

  Pipeline:
    01 SOQL query (filters 1..5 enforced at SF)
      -> dedupe by AccountId (group each owner's cases; preserve earliest
        future check-in as primary)
      -> Phase 1 filter: keep only accounts with count(cases) == 1
      -> loop over single-date accounts
          |-- fetch contact for account
          +-- shape eligible-callable row
      -> join with primary case context
      -> response

  Phase 1 single-date filter keeps only accounts that have a single open
  date in the future so that we don't have to handle multiple dates.
  Phase 2 will replace this with a chronological loop across an owner's
  future dates.

trigger:
  type: manual
  input_schema:
    # Optional check-in date window. Forwarded into soql-open-cases's
    # SOQL via the subflow input below. Defaults live in soql-open-cases's
    # normalizer node, so leaving these blank (or omitting them entirely)
    # reproduces the original ``>= TODAY`` behavior.
    check_in_start:
      type: string
      required: false
    check_in_end:
      type: string
      required: false

connections:
  salesforce:
    provider_config_key: salesforce
    connection_id: 70959a87-160b-439b-967f-aa0146c4b112

nodes:
  - id: 1
    type: subflow
    title: Get open ResCon cases (SOQL)
    workflow_file: ../_atoms/soql-open-cases/workflow.yaml
    # Forward the optional date window. The normalizer handles the
    # undefined-or-empty -> default fallback, so passing {{ input.* }}
    # verbatim here is safe even when the caller omits either key.
    input:
      check_in_start: "{{ input.check_in_start }}"
      check_in_end:   "{{ input.check_in_end }}"

  - id: 2
    type: transform
    title: Dedupe cases by AccountId
    description: |
      SOQL (soql-open-cases) ORDERs BY AccountId, Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c
      ASC — so the first row per owner is their earliest future check-in,
      which we keep as ``primary_case``. The full list of this owner's cases
      is preserved under ``cases`` so Phase 1 can count them and Phase 2
      can iterate over them in order.
    input:
      rows: "{{ nodes.1.output.records }}"
    # Outer [] forces array context — JSONata's $reduce over an empty
    # list returns the accumulator [], but some template resolvers trip
    # on the mixed-type edge case. Wrapping keeps it uniformly a list.
    expression: |
      [$reduce(rows, function($acc, $row) {(
        $existing := $acc[AccountId = $row.AccountId];
        $exists($existing)
          ? $append($acc[AccountId != $row.AccountId], {
              "AccountId":    $row.AccountId,
              "account_name": $row."Account"."Name",
              "primary_case": $existing.primary_case,
              "cases":        $append($existing.cases, $row)
            })
          : $append($acc, {
              "AccountId":    $row.AccountId,
              "account_name": $row."Account"."Name",
              "primary_case": $row,
              "cases":        [$row]
            })
      )}, [])]

  - id: 3
    type: transform
    title: Phase 1 — keep accounts with a single future check-in date
    description: |
      Phase 1 handles single-date owners only; multi-date owners are
      deferred to Phase 2.

      Definition of "single date": count of DISTINCT
      ``Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c`` values across this owner's open
      cases == 1. We filter on ``$count($distinct(...))`` (not raw
      ``$count(cases)``) because an owner can legitimately have 2+
      cases on the SAME check-in date — e.g., one primary reservation
      plus a linked guest-ticket case — and that's still ONE date to
      handle. The SOQL already restricted to ``>= TODAY`` so every
      date in the array is a future date by construction.

      Multi-date owners (distinct dates > 1) are NOT silently dropped
      on purpose; they're surfaced in ``deferred_multi_date`` so the
      run detail page shows the Phase 2 backlog explicitly. The dial
      fan-out only uses ``eligible``.
    input:
      accounts: "{{ nodes.2.output }}"
    expression: |
      {
        "eligible":             [accounts[$count($distinct(cases.Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c)) = 1]],
        "deferred_multi_date":  [accounts[$count($distinct(cases.Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c)) > 1]]
      }

  - id: 4
    type: loop
    title: For each eligible account, fetch contact + shape row
    iterate_over: "{{ nodes.3.output.eligible }}"
    item_alias: account
    concurrency: 5
    body:
      type: subflow
      workflow_file: ../_atoms/contact-for-account/workflow.yaml
      input:
        account_id: "{{ account.AccountId }}"
    collect:
      account_id:   "{{ account.AccountId }}"
      account_name: "{{ account.account_name }}"
      contact_id:   "{{ subflow.output.contact_id }}"
      first_name:   "{{ subflow.output.first_name }}"
      last_name:    "{{ subflow.output.last_name }}"
      phone:        "{{ subflow.output.phone }}"
      email:        "{{ subflow.output.email }}"

  - id: 5
    type: transform
    title: Join eligible callables with their primary case context
    description: |
      Each eligible account has exactly one case (Phase 1 invariant), so
      we merge that case's fields onto the contact-enriched row produced
      by the loop. Field names on the left match the downstream
      ``batch_call.variables`` envelope in the orchestrator.

      Person Account fallbacks: the Contact lookup (node 4) may return
      null fields when the Account is a Person Account with no separate
      Contact record or no Phone on the Contact. We fall back to
      Case-level fields (case_cell_phone, case_home_phone, case_work_phone
      for phone; Person_Account_Email__c for email) and split
      Account.Name for first/last name.
    input:
      eligible_callables: "{{ nodes.4.output }}"
      phase1_eligible:    "{{ nodes.3.output.eligible }}"
    # Outer [] — same array-context guard as node 2.
    expression: |
      (
        /* Strip phone extensions (x, ext, #), non-digit chars, and normalize
           to E.164. US 10-digit numbers get +1 prefix. Numbers already in
           E.164 (+...) pass through. Returns null for empty input.
           "874-423-9064x3109" -> "+18744239064"
           "530-680-3341"      -> "+15306803341"
           "+917732924082"     -> "+917732924082" (non-US, already E.164) */
        $clean_phone := function($raw) {(
          $stripped := $raw ? $replace($raw, /[xX#].*$/, "") : null;
          $digits := $stripped ? $replace($stripped, /[^\d+]/, "") : null;
          $digits = null or $digits = "" ? null
            : $substring($digits, 0, 1) = "+" ? $digits
            : $length($digits) = 10 ? "+1" & $digits
            : $length($digits) = 11 and $substring($digits, 0, 1) = "1" ? "+" & $digits
            : "+" & $digits
        )};

        /* First non-null cleaned phone from the priority list.
           Order: Case Home -> Case Cell -> Case Work -> Account Phone -> Account Mobile. */
        $pick_phone := function($phones) {(
          $cleaned := $map($phones, $clean_phone);
          $reduce($cleaned, function($acc, $p) {
            $acc ? $acc : ($p and $p != "" ? $p : null)
          }, null)
        )};

        [$map(eligible_callables, function($e) {(
          $acct := phase1_eligible[AccountId = $e.account_id];
          $case := $acct.primary_case;
          $res_type := $case."SPI_Reservation__r" ? $case."SPI_Reservation__r"."Reservation_Type__c" : null;
          $owner_type := $case.Account_Owner_Type__c;
          $name_parts := $split($e.account_name, " ");
          $resolved_phone := $pick_phone([
            $case.Case_Home_Phone__c,
            $case.Case_Cell_Phone__c,
            $case.Case_Work_Phone__c,
            $e.phone,
            $case."Account"."Phone",
            $case."Account"."PersonMobilePhone"
          ]);
          $merge([$e, {
            "case_id":         $case.Id,
            "case_number":     $case.CaseNumber,
            "subject":         $case.Subject,
            "city":            $case.City__c,
            "check_in_date":   $case.Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c,
            "check_out_date":  $case.WaitList_Check_Out_Date__c,
            "resort":          $case."Related_GPR_Resort__r" ? $case."Related_GPR_Resort__r"."Name" : $case.Resort_Name__c,
            "contract_id":     $case.Certificate_Number__c,
            "sum_past_due":    $case.Total_Amount_Due__c,
            "case_home_phone": $case.Case_Home_Phone__c,
            "case_cell_phone": $case.Case_Cell_Phone__c,
            "case_work_phone": $case.Case_Work_Phone__c,
            "case_email":      $case.Person_Account_Email__c,
            "first_name":      $e.first_name ? $e.first_name : $name_parts[0],
            "last_name":       $e.last_name ? $e.last_name : ($count($name_parts) > 1 ? $join($filter($name_parts, function($v, $i) { $i > 0 }), " ") : ""),
            "phone":           $resolved_phone,
            "email":           $e.email ? $e.email : $case.Person_Account_Email__c,
            "account_owner_type": $owner_type,
            "reservation_type": $res_type,
            "customer_type":   $res_type = "Owner"   ? "owner"
                             : $res_type = "Exchange" ? "exchanger"
                             : $res_type = "Walk-In"  ? "guest"
                             :                          "owner"
          }])
        )})]
      )

  - id: 6
    type: transform
    title: Compute Phase 1 summary counts
    description: |
      JSONata ``$count`` aggregates live here (not in node 7's response
      body), because the response node's ``{{ }}`` template engine is
      string-interpolation only — it doesn't evaluate JSONata function
      calls. Computing the counts in a transform lets node 7 just
      reference ``nodes.6.output.*`` literals.
    input:
      records:  "{{ nodes.1.output.records }}"
      deduped:  "{{ nodes.2.output }}"
      eligible: "{{ nodes.3.output.eligible }}"
      deferred: "{{ nodes.3.output.deferred_multi_date }}"
    expression: |
      {
        "total_soql_matches":  $count(records),
        "distinct_accounts":   $count(deduped),
        "phase1_eligible":     $count(eligible),
        "deferred_multi_date": $count(deferred)
      }

  - id: 7
    type: response
    title: Return eligible callables (+ Phase 2 backlog)
    body:
      # Primary output — the dialable rows fed to batch_call.
      callables: "{{ nodes.5.output }}"
      # Visibility into the Phase 1 gate (precomputed in node 6).
      counts: "{{ nodes.6.output }}"
      # Surface (not dial) the multi-date backlog so ops can see Phase 2 demand.
      deferred_multi_date: "{{ nodes.3.output.deferred_multi_date }}"

edges:
  - from: 1
    to: 2
  - from: 2
    to: 3
  - from: 3
    to: 4
  - from: 4
    to: 5
  - from: 5
    to: 6
  - from: 6
    to: 7

results.name: close-case-confirmed
results.description:
Post-call writeback for the ANSWERED / Confirmed branch.

Mirrors the legacy post-call logging flow — the positive
(outcome=completed) path:

  1. POST /sobjects/VoiceCall  -> create VoiceCall record linked to the
     Case + Account, with all wrap-up fields (call source, disposition,
     resort, summary, transcript, detail).
  2. PATCH /sobjects/Case/{id} -> flip Case to Closed with
     Resolution_Code__c = "RC: Confirmed" and
     Case_Resolution_Detail_v2__c = "RC: Will Use".

Both calls are made via Nango Proxy with the existing salesforce
connection (sibling pattern with soql-open-cases). No conditional logic
inside this atom — the branch decision is handled one level up in
../post-call-pipeline/.

Field surface mirrors the production VoiceCall body verbatim so the
resulting SF record is equivalent to what the legacy system creates.
results.definition_yaml:
name: close_case_confirmed
description: |
  Post-call writeback for the ANSWERED / Confirmed branch.

  Mirrors the legacy post-call logging flow — the positive
  (outcome=completed) path:

    1. POST /sobjects/VoiceCall  -> create VoiceCall record linked to the
       Case + Account, with all wrap-up fields (call source, disposition,
       resort, summary, transcript, detail).
    2. PATCH /sobjects/Case/{id} -> flip Case to Closed with
       Resolution_Code__c = "RC: Confirmed" and
       Case_Resolution_Detail_v2__c = "RC: Will Use".

  Both calls are made via Nango Proxy with the existing salesforce
  connection (sibling pattern with soql-open-cases). No conditional logic
  inside this atom — the branch decision is handled one level up in
  ../post-call-pipeline/.

  Field surface mirrors the production VoiceCall body verbatim so the
  resulting SF record is equivalent to what the legacy system creates.

trigger:
  type: manual
  input_schema:
    # Identifiers — flow upstream from the orchestrator's batch_call
    # result envelope.
    case_id:
      type: string
      required: true
    account_id:
      type: string
      required: true

    # VoiceCall metadata — supplied by voice-py / Twilio side of the dial.
    from_phone:
      type: string
      required: true
    to_phone:
      type: string
      required: true
    call_timestamp:
      # ISO 8601, used for both CallStartDateTime and CallEndDateTime.
      # The legacy system uses the same value for both (instantaneous
      # record at the end of the dial); preserve that semantic until we
      # have a real start/end pair to wire in.
      type: string
      required: true
    call_sid:
      # Twilio CallSid -> SF VoiceCall.VendorCallKey (idempotency anchor).
      type: string
      required: true
    call_center_id:
      # SF CallCenter.Id — resolved per-tenant. Pass through as input so
      # we can parametrize per env.
      type: string
      required: true

    # Wrap-up fields — populated by the agent / summarizer post-call.
    resort:
      # Pass the Case.Resort_Name__c LABEL verbatim (what voice-py
      # surfaces to the agent). Node 1 coerces LABEL -> VC picklist
      # VALUE before the POST because ``VoiceCall.GPR_Resort__c`` is a
      # restricted picklist that matches on value, not label.
      type: string
      required: true
    summary:
      type: string
      required: true
    transcript:
      # Full agent/customer transcript. Kept for future use (e.g. a
      # dedicated Transcript__c field). Currently NOT written to Description.
      type: string
      required: false
    description:
      # Formatted collected_variables (key: value per line). Written to
      # VoiceCall.Description so SF ops can see what the bot collected.
      type: string
      required: true
    call_source:
      type: string
      default: "OS"
      required: false
    call_disposition:
      type: string
      default: "OS: ResCon"
      required: false
    call_detail_1:
      type: string
      default: "OS: ResCon Success"
      required: false

constants:
  # ResCon outbound is always Outbound, never Inbound. Kept as a constant
  # rather than an input to make accidental misuse (e.g. inbound SF
  # writeback) harder.
  call_type: "Outbound"
  vendor_type: "Custom"

connections:
  salesforce:
    provider_config_key: salesforce
    connection_id: 70959a87-160b-439b-967f-aa0146c4b112

nodes:
  - id: 1
    type: transform
    # RESORT COERCION — cross-picklist translation from Case to VoiceCall.
    #
    # ``VoiceCall.GPR_Resort__c`` is a RESTRICTED picklist. The SF REST
    # API validates POST bodies against the picklist VALUE, not the
    # LABEL.
    #
    # Divergences between Case.Resort_Name__c and VC.GPR_Resort__c
    # (each would trigger INVALID_OR_NULL_FOR_RESTRICTED_PICKLIST):
    #
    #   1. Label/value split — same resort, different stored string:
    #        "Red Wolf Lodge at Olympic Valley" -> "Red Wolf Lodge at Squaw Valley"
    #   2. Typo-ish — singular/plural + "Resort" suffix drift:
    #        "Makai Club Resort Condo" -> "Makai Club Resort Condos"
    #        "Lea Casa"                -> "Lea Casa Resort"
    #   3. Case-only concepts with no VC analog — GPX wholesale brand,
    #      Grand Pacific Palisades variants, and the parent brand
    #      "Grand Pacific Resorts". We don't guess at a mapping here
    #      (picking the wrong resort pollutes SF reporting); they fall
    #      through to the "Not Applicable" fallback below.
    #
    # Fix shape:
    #   a. $resortAliases  — explicit map for the high-confidence renames.
    #   b. $vcResortValues — snapshot of the VC restricted picklist
    #      VALUES. Anything NOT in this set is replaced with
    #      "Not Applicable" so the POST never violates the picklist guard.
    #   c. Label is preserved on the response for debuggability.
    title: Coerce Case resort -> VC picklist value (with Not Applicable fallback)
    input:
      resort: "{{ input.resort }}"
    expression: |
      (
        $resortAliases := {
          "Red Wolf Lodge at Olympic Valley": "Red Wolf Lodge at Squaw Valley",
          "Makai Club Resort Condo":          "Makai Club Resort Condos",
          "Lea Casa":                         "Lea Casa Resort"
        };
        $vcResortValues := [
          "Not Applicable",
          "Canyon Rim at Eagle Crest",
          "Capistrano Surfside Inn",
          "Carlsbad Inn Beach Resort",
          "Carlsbad Seapointe Resort",
          "Channel Island Shores",
          "Circle J Club",
          "Club Tahoe Resort",
          "Coronado Beach Resort",
          "French Lick Villas",
          "Grand Pacific Palisades Resort",
          "Grand Pacific Vacation Club",
          "Hanalei Bay Resort",
          "Hilton Grand Vacations Club at MarBrisa",
          "Indian Palms Vacation Club",
          "Lea Casa Resort",
          "Makai Club Resort Condos",
          "Makai Club Resort Cottages",
          "Olympic Village Inn",
          "Red Wolf Lakeside Lodge",
          "Red Wolf Lodge at Squaw Valley",
          "RiverPointe Napa Valley",
          "San Clemente Cove",
          "San Clemente Inn",
          "San Diego Country Estates",
          "Southern California Beach Club",
          "Tahoe Sands Resort",
          "Villa L'Auberge",
          "Vista Mirage Resort",
          "Wave Crest Resort"
        ];
        $aliased   := $lookup($resortAliases, resort);
        $candidate := $aliased ? $aliased : resort;
        $final     := $candidate in $vcResortValues ? $candidate : "Not Applicable";
        {
          "resort_label":    resort,
          "resort_aliased":  $aliased,
          "resort_candidate": $candidate,
          "resort_value":    $final
        }
      )

  - id: 2
    type: nango_proxy
    # Create the VoiceCall record. SF returns { id, success, errors[] };
    # we surface the id so the caller can correlate the SFDC VC PK with
    # our internal call run.
    title: Create VoiceCall record (linked to Case + Account)
    provider: salesforce
    method: POST
    endpoint: /services/data/v59.0/sobjects/VoiceCall
    body:
      CallType:           "{{ constants.call_type }}"
      FromPhoneNumber:    "{{ input.from_phone }}"
      ToPhoneNumber:      "{{ input.to_phone }}"
      CallStartDateTime:  "{{ input.call_timestamp }}"
      CallEndDateTime:    "{{ input.call_timestamp }}"
      VendorCallKey:      "{{ input.call_sid }}"
      VendorType:         "{{ constants.vendor_type }}"
      CallCenterId:       "{{ input.call_center_id }}"
      Case__c:            "{{ input.case_id }}"
      Account__c:         "{{ input.account_id }}"
      Call_Source__c:     "{{ input.call_source }}"
      Call_Disposition__c: "{{ input.call_disposition }}"
      # Use the coerced picklist VALUE from node 1, NOT the raw label
      # in input.resort. See node 1's comment block for why.
      GPR_Resort__c:      "{{ nodes.1.output.resort_value }}"
      Summary__c:         "{{ input.summary }}"
      Description:        "{{ input.description }}"
      Call_Detail_1__c:   "{{ input.call_detail_1 }}"

  - id: 3
    type: nango_proxy
    # Flip the Case to Closed with the Confirmed resolution. SF returns
    # 204 No Content; the proxy surfaces status (no body).
    title: Close Case as Confirmed (RC=Confirmed, Detail=Will Use)
    provider: salesforce
    method: PATCH
    endpoint: "/services/data/v59.0/sobjects/Case/{{ input.case_id }}"
    body:
      Status: "Closed"
      Resolution_Code__c: "RC: Confirmed"
      Case_Resolution_Detail_v2__c: "RC: Will Use"

  - id: 4
    type: response
    title: Return wrap-up summary
    body:
      voice_call_id:    "{{ nodes.2.body.id }}"
      voice_call_ok:    "{{ nodes.2.body.success }}"
      case_id:          "{{ input.case_id }}"
      case_close_status: "{{ nodes.3.status }}"
      branch:           "confirmed"

edges:
  - from: 1
    to: 2
  - from: 2
    to: 3
  - from: 3
    to: 4

results.name: close-case-no-contact
results.description:
Post-call writeback for the NO-CONTACT / Unconfirmed branch.

Mirrors the legacy post-call logging flow — the negative
(outcome != "completed") path:

  PATCH /sobjects/Case/{id} -> flip Case to Closed with
  Resolution_Code__c = "RC: Unconfirmed" and
  Case_Resolution_Detail_v2__c = "RC: No Contact".

No VoiceCall record is created in this branch — only calls that reach
a person get a VC record. If/when we adopt the "No Contact Case
Closure & Email" enrichment (Comments + OwnerId reassignment +
agent-routed email), extend this atom; the optional inputs
``agent_notes`` and ``agent_id`` are reserved for that.
results.definition_yaml:
name: close_case_no_contact
description: |
  Post-call writeback for the NO-CONTACT / Unconfirmed branch.

  Mirrors the legacy post-call logging flow — the negative
  (outcome != "completed") path:

    PATCH /sobjects/Case/{id} -> flip Case to Closed with
    Resolution_Code__c = "RC: Unconfirmed" and
    Case_Resolution_Detail_v2__c = "RC: No Contact".

  No VoiceCall record is created in this branch — only calls that reach
  a person get a VC record. If/when we adopt the "No Contact Case
  Closure & Email" enrichment (Comments + OwnerId reassignment +
  agent-routed email), extend this atom; the optional inputs
  ``agent_notes`` and ``agent_id`` are reserved for that.

trigger:
  type: manual
  input_schema:
    case_id:
      type: string
      required: true
    # Optional enrichments — currently unused by the minimal close-only
    # behavior. When set, body is extended with Comments and OwnerId so
    # the SF record includes the richer no-contact payload.
    agent_notes:
      type: string
      required: false
    agent_id:
      type: string
      required: false

connections:
  salesforce:
    provider_config_key: salesforce
    connection_id: 70959a87-160b-439b-967f-aa0146c4b112

nodes:
  - id: 1
    type: transform
    title: Build close payload (with optional enrichments)
    description: |
      JSONata builds the body conditionally: required fields always
      present; Comments/OwnerId only when their inputs are non-empty.
      Avoids sending null-valued fields to SF (they'd clobber existing
      values otherwise).
    input:
      agent_notes: "{{ input.agent_notes }}"
      agent_id:    "{{ input.agent_id }}"
    expression: |
      $merge([
        {
          "Status": "Closed",
          "Resolution_Code__c": "RC: Unconfirmed",
          "Case_Resolution_Detail_v2__c": "RC: No Contact"
        },
        agent_notes and agent_notes != "" ? { "Comments": agent_notes } : {},
        agent_id and agent_id != "" ? { "OwnerId": agent_id } : {}
      ])

  - id: 2
    type: nango_proxy
    title: Close Case as Unconfirmed/No Contact
    provider: salesforce
    method: PATCH
    endpoint: "/services/data/v59.0/sobjects/Case/{{ input.case_id }}"
    body: "{{ nodes.1.output }}"

  - id: 3
    type: response
    title: Return close summary
    body:
      voice_call_id:     null   # no VC created on this branch
      voice_call_ok:     false
      case_id:           "{{ input.case_id }}"
      case_close_status: "{{ nodes.2.status }}"
      branch:            "no_contact"

edges:
  - from: 1
    to: 2
  - from: 2
    to: 3

results.name: contact-for-account
results.description:
Fetch the first Contact with a phone number for a given AccountId.
This is the per-account lookup the agent does after the eligibility
gate passes — it gives the dialer a real phone number.
results.definition_yaml:
name: contact_for_account
description: |
  Fetch the first Contact with a phone number for a given AccountId.
  This is the per-account lookup the agent does after the eligibility
  gate passes — it gives the dialer a real phone number.

trigger:
  type: manual
  input_schema:
    account_id:
      type: string
      required: true

connections:
  salesforce:
    provider_config_key: salesforce
    connection_id: 70959a87-160b-439b-967f-aa0146c4b112

nodes:
  - id: 1
    type: nango_proxy
    # Nango Proxy: GET https://api.nango.dev/proxy/{endpoint}
    title: Get Contact for Account
    provider: salesforce
    method: GET
    endpoint: /services/data/v59.0/query
    query:
      q: |
        SELECT Id, FirstName, LastName, Phone, Email
        FROM Contact
        WHERE AccountId = '{{ input.account_id }}'
          AND Phone != null
        LIMIT 1

  - id: 2
    type: response
    title: Flatten contact for downstream consumer
    body:
      contact_id:    "{{ nodes.1.body.records[0].Id }}"
      first_name:    "{{ nodes.1.body.records[0].FirstName }}"
      last_name:     "{{ nodes.1.body.records[0].LastName }}"
      phone:         "{{ nodes.1.body.records[0].Phone }}"
      email:         "{{ nodes.1.body.records[0].Email }}"

edges:
  - from: 1
    to: 2

results.name: soql-open-cases
results.description:
Fetch open ResCon Cases via SOQL that encodes the full pre-call contract
in code.

Filters (intersect of the customer's production ``ResCon_today`` list view
AND operational guidance):

  1. Reason = 'RC: ResCon'                         — ResCon workflow only
     (standard Case picklist; UI label "Case Reason"; NOT a __c field)
  2. Status IN ('Active','Open','Re-Opened')       — excludes Closed
  3. Total_Amount_Due__c <= 50 OR NULL             — Scribe $50 gate
  4. Subject NOT LIKE '%Will Use My Week, ...%'    — excludes WUMW records
  5. Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c >= TODAY            — past dates should be
                                                     skipped because those
                                                     reservations have
                                                     already been missed.

Filters 1-4 mirror the 6-filter set on the ``ResCon_today`` saved list
view. Filter 5 is NOT on that view — past dates should be skipped but
the SF view owner hasn't added it. Enforcing it here keeps us correct
regardless of list-view drift.

Phase 1 scope: only Accounts with exactly one future open date are
callable. Accounts with multiple future dates are deferred to Phase 2
(chronological looping). That grouping happens in ``pre-call-pipeline``
(transform node, post-dedupe) — keeping the SOQL single-shot so the
eligibility numerator (total matching rows) is still auditable vs the
final dial count.
results.definition_yaml:
name: soql_open_cases
description: |
  Fetch open ResCon Cases via SOQL that encodes the full pre-call contract
  in code.

  Filters (intersect of the customer's production ``ResCon_today`` list view
  AND operational guidance):

    1. Reason = 'RC: ResCon'                         — ResCon workflow only
       (standard Case picklist; UI label "Case Reason"; NOT a __c field)
    2. Status IN ('Active','Open','Re-Opened')       — excludes Closed
    3. Total_Amount_Due__c <= 50 OR NULL             — Scribe $50 gate
    4. Subject NOT LIKE '%Will Use My Week, ...%'    — excludes WUMW records
    5. Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c >= TODAY            — past dates should be
                                                       skipped because those
                                                       reservations have
                                                       already been missed.

  Filters 1-4 mirror the 6-filter set on the ``ResCon_today`` saved list
  view. Filter 5 is NOT on that view — past dates should be skipped but
  the SF view owner hasn't added it. Enforcing it here keeps us correct
  regardless of list-view drift.

  Phase 1 scope: only Accounts with exactly one future open date are
  callable. Accounts with multiple future dates are deferred to Phase 2
  (chronological looping). That grouping happens in ``pre-call-pipeline``
  (transform node, post-dedupe) — keeping the SOQL single-shot so the
  eligibility numerator (total matching rows) is still auditable vs the
  final dial count.

trigger:
  type: manual
  input_schema:
    # Optional check-in date window. Both bounds accept SOQL date
    # literals (``TODAY``, ``NEXT_N_DAYS:30``, etc.) OR ISO date
    # strings (``2026-06-01``). Values interpolate into the SOQL WHERE
    # clause verbatim — no quotes added — so stick to things SOQL
    # accepts unquoted.
    #
    # Defaults (applied by node 1's normalizer when a caller omits
    # them or passes an empty string) keep production-scheduler
    # behavior identical to the pre-parameterization version:
    # ``>= TODAY`` with no practical upper bound.
    check_in_start:
      type: string
      required: false
      default: "TODAY"
    check_in_end:
      type: string
      required: false
      default: "2099-12-31"

connections:
  salesforce:
    provider_config_key: salesforce
    connection_id: 70959a87-160b-439b-967f-aa0146c4b112

nodes:
  - id: 1
    type: transform
    title: Normalize date window (fill in defaults)
    description: |
      Defaults live HERE (not only in ``input_schema.default``) so
      they apply uniformly across all three trigger paths — the
      Temporal scheduler's trigger-input-override JSON, the UI Run
      dialog, and any subflow caller that may omit the keys entirely.
      An undefined or empty-string input lands as ``TODAY`` /
      ``2099-12-31`` before the nango_proxy interpolates them into
      the SOQL string. Keeping this a small transform means only one
      place to reason about "what happens if the caller didn't pass
      dates?".
    input:
      start: "{{ input.check_in_start }}"
      end:   "{{ input.check_in_end }}"
    expression: |
      {
        "check_in_start": start and start != "" ? start : "TODAY",
        "check_in_end":   end   and end   != "" ? end   : "2099-12-31"
      }

  - id: 2
    type: nango_proxy
    # Nango Proxy: GET https://api.nango.dev/proxy/services/data/v59.0/query
    # Runtime handles OAuth token refresh + instance URL routing + query
    # string encoding transparently. SF REST returns:
    #   { totalSize: N, done: bool, records: [ {...flat case fields...} ] }
    # Unlike the list-view endpoint (which wraps each record in a
    # {columns:[{fieldNameOrPath,value}]} envelope), /query returns fields
    # as plain JSON keys — no flatten step needed downstream.
    title: Query open ResCon cases (SOQL)
    provider: salesforce
    method: GET
    endpoint: /services/data/v59.0/query
    query:
      q: |
        SELECT Id, CaseNumber, AccountId, Account.Name,
               Account.GPX_Exchange__c, Account.Phone, Account.PersonMobilePhone,
               Account_Owner_Type__c,
               SPI_Reservation__r.Reservation_Type__c,
               Subject, Status, Reason,
               Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c, WaitList_Check_Out_Date__c,
               Total_Amount_Due__c, City__c,
               Case_Home_Phone__c, Case_Cell_Phone__c, Case_Work_Phone__c,
               Person_Account_Email__c,
               Resort_Name__c, Related_GPR_Resort__r.Name,
               Certificate_Number__c
        FROM Case
        WHERE Reason = 'RC: ResCon'
          AND Status IN ('Active', 'Open', 'Re-Opened')
          AND (Total_Amount_Due__c <= 50 OR Total_Amount_Due__c = NULL)
          AND (NOT Subject LIKE '%Will Use My Week, Please confirm%')
          AND Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c >= {{ nodes.1.output.check_in_start }}
          AND Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c <= {{ nodes.1.output.check_in_end }}
        ORDER BY AccountId, Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c ASC

  - id: 3
    type: response
    title: Return SOQL query result
    body: "{{ nodes.2.body }}"

edges:
  - from: 1
    to: 2
  - from: 2
    to: 3

definition_parsed.name: soql_open_cases
